| Largest win           = {{fb|COM}} 7–1 {{fb-rt|SEY}}<br />([[Mitsamiouli]], [[Comoros]]; 1 September 2021)
| Largest loss          = {{flagicon|Réunion}} [[Réunion national football team|Réunion]] 6–1 {{fb-rt|Comoros|1978}}<br />([[Saint-Denis, Réunion]]; 31 August 1979)<br />{{fb|MAD}} 4–1 {{fb-rt|Comoros|1992}}<br />([[Victoria, Seychelles]]; 20 August 1993)<br />{{fb|MRI}} 4–1 {{fb-rt|Comoros}}<br />([[Curepipe]], [[Mauritius]]; 4 September 2003)

| 3rdRegional name      = [[COSAFA Cup]]

|medaltemplates={{MedalCompetition|[[COSAFA Cup]]}}
{{MedalBronze|[[2025 COSAFA Cup|2025 South Africa]]|Team}}
}}

The '''Comoros national football team''' represents the [[Comoros]] in international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Comoros Football Federation]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Vickers |first=Steve |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8323818.stm |title=BBC SPORT &#124; Football &#124; African &#124; Comoros make steps forward |work=BBC News |date=2009-10-24 |access-date=2013-12-03 |archive-date=7 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191207205731/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/football/africa/8323818.stm |url-status=live }}</ref> It was formed in 1979, joined the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF) in 2003, and became a [[FIFA]] member in 2005. Comoros qualified for their first major tournament in 2021, after their 0–0 draw with [[Togo national football team|Togo]] assured them of a place in the [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations]].<ref name=AFCON20201>{{cite news |title=Comoros achieve fairytale Nations Cup qualification |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/africa/56528169 |access-date=25 March 2021 |work=BBC Sport |date=25 March 2021 |archive-date=25 March 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210325150807/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/africa/56528169 |url-status=live }}</ref>

==History==
The national football team of the Comoros played their first matches in the [[Football at the 1979 Indian Ocean Island Games|1979 Indian Ocean Island Games]]. Those were the only games they played until [[2009 Arab Nations Cup#Group 1|qualification for the 2009 Arab Nations Cup]] in 2006. Those two matches were their first two matches with full FIFA recognition, and included a 4–2 victory over [[Djibouti national football team|Djibouti]]. In 2007, Comoros first entered qualification for the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] and the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] for the 2010 tournaments, but lost in the preliminary round 2–10 on aggregate to [[Madagascar national football team|Madagascar]].

During the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF first round|2018 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]] in October 2015, ''Les Coelacantes'' advanced past the first round for the first time ever by beating [[Lesotho national football team|Lesotho]] on away goals after two draws. Since then results have improved steadily, including home wins against [[Botswana national football team|Botswana]], [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]] and [[Malawi national football team|Malawi]] in Africa Cup of Nations qualifiers.

On 14 November 2019, Comoros opened their [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]] campaign with a 0–1 win away to [[Togo national football team|Togo]], their first away win in a major qualifier. They would go on to qualify for the [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|final tournament]], their first major tournament since joining FIFA.<ref name=AFCON20201 />

In the [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations]], [[Ahmed Mogni]] scored a brace for Comoros in their 3–2 group match victory over [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]] in what the BBC described as "one of the biggest shocks in Nations Cup history".<ref>{{Cite news|first=Rob|last=Stevens|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/59947164|title=Ghana dumped out of Afcon by Comoros|publisher=BBC Sport|date=18 January 2022|access-date=9 February 2022|archive-date=27 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220127093059/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/59947164|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2026 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], Comoros started off strong within [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group I|Group I]] after they won 4–2 against [[Central African Republic national football team|Central African Republic]] and 1–0 against [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]], with both games being played at home.<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 November 2023 |title=World Cup 2026 qualifying: Ghana shocked by Comoros, Senegal and Cameroon held |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/africa/67486638 |access-date=23 November 2023 |website=BBC News |archive-date=22 November 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231122145355/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/africa/67486638 |url-status=live }}</ref> Comoros then won 2-0 against [[Chad national football team|Chad]], but they also lost 2-1 to [[Madagascar national football team|Madagascar]]. They would be eliminated after losing to Madagascar 2-1 again.

During the [[2024 COSAFA Cup]], Comoros reached the semi-finals for the first time in their history and lost 2-1 against [[Angola national football team|Angola]]; Comoros then claimed fourth place after losing on [[penalty shootout|penalties]] against [[Mozambique national football team|Mozambique]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Comoros vs. Mozambique 2024-07-07 |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/matches/report/39289/Comoros_Mozambique.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref> Comoros went one better in the [[2025 COSAFA Cup]] and finished third after defeating [[Madagascar national football team|Madagascar]] 1–0.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Madagascar 0:1 Comoros |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2025-06-15/madagascar-vs-comoros/3734016/ |website=Global Sports Archive}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Comoros national football team results (2020–present)}}

*{{pengoal}} [[Akim Abdallah|Abdallah]]
*{{pengoal}} [[Raouf Mroivili|Mroivili]]

| stadium    = [[Astana Arena]]
| attendance = 
| referee    = [[Arman Ismuratov]] ([[Kazakhstan Football Federation|Kazakhstan]])
| result     = D
}}

== Coaching staff ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Position
! Name
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Hubert Velud]]
|-
|Assistant coaches
|{{flagicon|COM}} Mostoifa Ahmed Ben Hassane<br>{{flagicon|COM}} Cheikh Mohamed Abubakar
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|COM}} Faisal Soilihi
|-
|Fitness coach
|{{flagicon|COM}} Youssouf Omar
|-
|Match analyst
|{{flagicon|COM}} Said Hamadi
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|COM}} [[Ali Mbaé Camara]]
|-
|}

===Coaching history===
{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Pierre Jacky]] (1985)
*{{flagicon|COM}} [[Ali Mbaé Camara]] (2006–2007)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Manuel Amoros]] (2010)
*{{flagicon|COM}} [[Mohamed Chamité]] (2010–2011)
*{{flagicon|COM}} [[Ali Mbaé Camara]] (2011–2013)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Amir Abdou]] (2014–2022)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Younes Zerdouk]] (2022–2023) 
*{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Stefano Cusin]] (2023–2026)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Hubert Velud]] (2026–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]] between 21 December 2025 and 18 January 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/permalink.php?story_fbid=pfbid0umRqrqzYQjHxoQ8CNdutuRkw8LAg1o1KLuzWJRhCc554YVYFRoup3WxeT4L1KQgil&id=100083085443334|title=𝐋𝐈𝐒𝐓𝐄 𝐎𝐅𝐅𝐈𝐂𝐈𝐄𝐋𝐋𝐄 𝐂𝐎𝐄𝐋𝐀𝐂𝐀𝐍𝐓𝐇𝐄𝐒 𝐂𝐀𝐍 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓|via=Facebook|publisher=[[Comoros Football Federation]]|date=9 December 2025|language=fr}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 29 December 2025, after the match against [[Mali national football team|Mali]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Salim Ben Boina]]|age={{Bda|df=yes|1991|7|19}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[Istres FC|Istres]]|clubnat=FRA}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|29 December 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Zimmerman |first1=Benjamin |title=Comoros |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/222/Comoros.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with the Comoros.''

|2015–present

|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Mohamed Youssouf]]'''
|3
|46
|{{#expr:3/46 round 2}}
|2011–present
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"

!Position

|-

|-

|-

|-

|-
|{{flagicon|Japan}} [[Football at the 2020 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2020]]
|-
|{{flagicon|France}} [[Football at the 2024 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2024]]
|-
!Total|| ||0/28|| || || || || ||
|}
*<small>Football at the Summer Olympics has been an under-23 tournament since the 1992 edition.</small>

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[Football at the Indian Ocean Island Games|Indian Ocean Island Games]]'''
** {{Bronze3}} Bronze Medal (2): [[Football at the 1979 Indian Ocean Island Games|1979]], 1985
